analysis.note: Formally also Dvitiya Eka; construed here as Prathama Eka because it is a listed item under kuśalāḥ.

verification.detail: compound stem alobhāditraya as Napumsaka/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Formally also Dvitiya Eka; construed as Prathama Eka in the nominative enumeration.
Read as one sa-apramādika compound, an enumerative adjective agreeing by proximity with praśrabdhiḥ: ‘together with those beginning with apramāda’.

verification.detail: compound stem sāpramādika as Stri/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation bahuvrihi: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: The supplied bhāṣya reading has long initial ā; semantically this is the non-harm term more commonly normalized as ahiṃsā.
Substantivized masculine plural: ‘wholesome factors / wholesome ones’; it does not directly modify the preceding feminine singular āhiṃsā.
Pausal visarga is lost before the following voiced r in sandhi.

samasa.vigraha: rāgaś ca pratighaś ca mūḍhiś ca

analysis.note: The ending -ayaḥ is the Prathama Bahu of the feminine i-stem final member mūḍhi; the compound as a whole is an enumerative dvandva of kleśa-names.

verification.detail: compound stem rāgapratighamūḍhi as Stri/Prathama/Bahu: pass; relation dvandva: unsupported (logged)

justifications.decision: Treat the first expression as a single neuter nominative compound headed by traya, not as three independent listed words.
options_considered: single compound: alobhādi-trayam, ‘triad beginning with alobha’
three separate words: alobha, ādi, trayam

evidence.translation: Grammatically, trayaṃ is the neuter singular head, while alobha-ādi functions as ‘beginning with non-greed’; in the nominative list under kuśalāḥ this yields ‘the triad beginning with non-greed’.

justifications.decision: Do not take kuśalāḥ as an adjective modifying āhiṃsā; it labels the whole preceding set.
options_considered: kuśalāḥ is a substantivized predicate/heading for the listed items
justifications.chosen: kuśalāḥ is a substantivized masculine plural label: ‘the wholesome factors’.

evidence.translation: āhiṃsā is feminine singular, whereas kuśalāḥ is masculine plural. The mismatch favors a substantivized plural, ‘wholesome ones/factors’, rather than direct adjectival agreement.

justifications.decision: Prefer the compound analysis over splitting sā + apramādikā as a pronoun plus adjective.
options_considered: sa-apramādikā modifying praśrabdhiḥ, ‘pliancy together with apramāda etc.’
sa-apramādikā modifying āhiṃsā across the pāda boundary
justifications.chosen: sāpramādikā is read as one sa-apramādika compound, ‘together with those beginning with apramāda’, agreeing with praśrabdhiḥ by proximity.

evidence.translation: Sandhi permits either sā + apramādikā or sa-apramādikā, but a separate demonstrative sā is syntactically idle in this compact list. A sa- compound gives the expected enumerative sense ‘with X and the rest’. Immediate adjacency favors agreement with praśrabdhiḥ, though the exact implicit members are not fixed by grammar alone.

justifications.decision: Translate it as a list of afflictions—desire, hostility, and delusion—rather than as a modifier of a single ‘delusions’ noun.
options_considered: tatpuruṣa: delusions characterized by rāga and pratigha
bahuvrīhi: those whose delusions are rāga and pratigha
justifications.chosen: rāgapratighamūḍhayaḥ is an itaretara-dvandva: rāga, pratigha, and mūḍhi.

evidence.translation: After the masculine plural heading kleśāḥ, the compound names members of the class. The final -ayaḥ points to feminine i-stem mūḍhi in nominative plural, and rāga/pratigha are natural kleśa-terms; MW also gives pratigha as anger/wrath/enmity in such contexts.

justifications.decision: Analyze the printed form rather than normalizing silently to the common ahiṃsā.
options_considered: retain and analyze āhiṃsā as printed
justifications.chosen: Keep the supplied reading āhiṃsā with long initial ā.

evidence.translation: The base text supplied for this run prints āhiṃsā. Although the standard lexical form is ahiṃsā, the apparatus records and translates the actual surface form.

translation:
The wholesome factors (kuśalāḥ) are:
the triad beginning with non-greed (alobhāditraya), energy (vīrya),
pliancy (praśrabdhi), together with those beginning with heedfulness (apramāda),
and non-harm (āhiṃsā).

The afflictions (kleśāḥ) are desire (rāga),
hostility (pratigha), and delusion (mūḍhi).
one_shot_delta: A too literal dictionary-first translation might render rāga as ‘color’ and pratigha as ‘obstruction’; the kleśa context instead calls for desire/passion and hostility/anger.
It might split sāpramādikā into sā + apramādikā and produce an awkward ‘she is beginning with non-carelessness’, rather than the enumerative ‘together with those beginning with apramāda’.
It might try to make kuśalāḥ modify āhiṃsā directly, missing the gender/number mismatch and the substantivized sense ‘wholesome factors’.
It might silently replace the printed āhiṃsā with the more common ahiṃsā without noting the reading.
analyzer_disagreements: Consolidated the analyzer’s alobha_ādi_trayam into one inflected compound, alobhāditrayam, with traya as the neuter singular head.
Read sāpramādikā as a single sa-apramādika compound rather than the analyzer’s possible split sā + apramādikā; this better fits the compact enumeration.
Analyzed āhiṃsā as an independent Stri Prathama Eka subanta, not merely as a compound pre-member label.
Consolidated rāga_pratigha_mūḍhayaḥ into one dvandva compound; retained the analyzer’s insight that the final member is feminine i-stem mūḍhi, Prathama Bahu.
open_questions: Without commentary, the precise members implied by -ādikā in sāpramādikā cannot be fixed from this verse alone.
The attachment of sāpramādikā to praśrabdhiḥ rather than to āhiṃsā is chosen by proximity and list-logic, but the bare grammar allows both feminine nouns.
The long initial ā of āhiṃsā is treated as the supplied reading; grammar alone does not decide whether it is orthographic, metrical, or a distinct textual form.
